title: Registry Publication, SBOM, Provenance, Vulnerabilities, Policies, and CI Release · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Registry Auth Push Pull
summary: A regulated service needs evidence for what an image contains, how it was built, which base it used, who approved it, and how rollback selects immutable content. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker registry auth push pull increment in signed and policy-gated image release packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Publish least-privilege authenticated content, verify digest after push, handle immutable tags, retention, mirrors, rate limits, and failure without credential leakage.
Define inherited Bake targets, variables, matrices, groups, contexts, cache, attestations, outputs, and policy while preventing accidental pushes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example candidate has a mutable tag, incomplete SBOM, minimal provenance, critical scanner finding, and unsigned exception. Bind source, base, builder, attestations, signature, policy, and promotion to a digest. Competency probe: Publish least-privilege authenticated content, verify digest after push, handle immutable tags, retention, mirrors, rate limits, and failure without credential leakage. A defensible solution applies Docker Registry Auth Push Pull, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to signed and policy-gated image release packet.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-supply-chain-registry-release-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-supply-chain-registry-release-3
    artifact: release-policy.yaml
    decision: worked docker supply chain registry release 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      source: reviewed-commit
      base: approved-digest
      output: immutable-registry-digest
      sbom: spdx-attestation
      provenance: max
      vulnerability-policy: severity-and-exploitability
      signature: identity-bound
    verify: docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate@sha256:release && docker scout cves candidate@sha256:release
    failure: Reject tag-only promotion, SBOM-as-vulnerability-scan, unsigned attestations, ignored base drift, or severity alone without reachability and exception ownership.
    repair: Bind source, builder, inputs, output digest, SBOM, provenance, signature, scan policy, exception expiry, and promotion evidence.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
